title: Fundamental Rights
description: Articles 12–35: rights guaranteed to every person in India.

questions.q: Under which article of the Constitution is the Right to Equality guaranteed?
questions.explanation: Article 14 guarantees the right to equality before law and equal protection of laws. It applies to all persons, not just citizens.

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar described which article as the "heart and soul of the Constitution"?
questions.explanation: Article 32 — the Right to Constitutional Remedies — was called the "heart and soul of the Constitution" by Dr. Ambedkar because it gives citizens the right to enforce their Fundamental Rights in the Supreme Court.

title: Preamble
description: The introductory statement of the Constitution and its key values.

questions.q: Which words were added to the Preamble by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment (1976)?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added "Socialist," "Secular," and "Integrity" to the Preamble.
